articles.title: How many constituencies can a candidate contest from? | TN Elections with The Hindu
articles.description:
In the 2001 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa filed nominations from four seats and 
all four nominations were rejected. Protests erupted. The ruling government was accused of conspiracy.
And ten years later, the Supreme Court pulled up that leader sharply. That leader was AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa. How many constituencies can a candidate legally contest from in India?

topStories.description: In the poll-bound State, PM Narendra Modi says plantation workers had to wait years for land rights because Congress never cared for them during their tenure; this is the first such initiative in 200 years of Assam tea

topStories.title: Who's Sameerah Munshi? Only Muslim Woman On White House Religious Liberty Commission Quits
topStories.description: Munshi criticised the Trump administration's actions in Iran, saying the US government was responsible for the unlawful killing of civilians and children.

topStories.title: Supreme Court declines plea to make nucleic acid tests compulsory at blood banks
topStories.description: NAT tests are more expensive but more accurate in detecting HIV, Hepatitis B, C viruses in the blood; petitioner sought to protect thalassemia patients who require frequent blood transfusions and are at risk from infected blood

topStories.title: Why Is The Dollar Profiting From Middle East War?
topStories.description: The surge in energy prices triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has significantly strengthened the dollar, paradoxically undermining US President Donald Trump's economic objectives.

topStories.title: Ukraine and allies fear easing Russian sanctions will prolong war
topStories.description: Ukraine's president says the US decision "certainly does not help achieve peace".

topStories.title: Thousands march worldwide in solidarity with Palestine, Iran on al-Quds Day
topStories.description: Despite war, al-Quds Day marches in Tehran saw thousands protest, voicing anger against Israel and the US.

dailyNews.title: What rats do’: US says Iranian leaders hiding as leaders appear in Tehran
dailyNews.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Iran’s leadership was “desperate and hiding,” after US-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: Haryana Rajya Sabha polls: Congress MLAs reach Himachal Pradesh to prevent cross-voting
dailyNews.description: After CLP meet, about 30 MLAs leave for Shimla; party insiders say the move is meant to keep the flock together ahead of three-cornered election; Congress holds 37 seats and needs 31 votes to send its candidate

dailyNews.title: Brazil’s jailed ex-President Jair Bolsonaro hospitalised with bronchopneumonia; condition serious
dailyNews.description: The 70-year-old was taken to the hospital early Friday (March 13) after experiencing high fever, chills and a drop in oxygen saturation, doctors said.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine, EU allies slam US decision to roll back Russia oil sanctions
dailyNews.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says move 'does not help peace' as Europe reels from soaring energy prices linked to Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine finds new role as protector of US, Gulf allies amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Having abandoned Ukraine, the US has sought Kyiv's support in defence from Iranian drones.

dailyNews.title: Sheriff in Nancy Guthrie case believes they know kidnapper's motive
dailyNews.description: The 84-year-old mother of US TV presenter Savannah Guthrie was kidnapped 41 days ago.

dailyNews.description: Despite war, al-Quds Day marches in Tehran saw thousands protest, voicing anger against Israel and the US.

dailyNews.title: A president suing himself? Why experts say Trump’s $10bn lawsuit might fail
dailyNews.description: Experts say Trump's decision to sue over his leaked tax returns risks putting him in charge of arranging his own payout.

dailyNews.title: Iran’s arsenal power crushed? US Defence Secy Pete Hegseth claims ‘missiles down 90%, drones down 95%’
dailyNews.description: Speaking at a Pentagon press conference, Hegseth said: “Iran has no air defenses. Iran has no air force. Iran has no navy. Their missiles, their missile launchers and drones are being destroyed…”

dailyNews.title: Chipotle announces BOGO burritos on Friday, 13 March, in US. But here's the catch to avail the offer — details here
dailyNews.description: Chipotle Mexican Grill is offering a limited-time BOGO deal on March 13, from 3-4 p.m. Customers must show a tattoo to redeem the offer, which allows one free item for every purchased entrée of equal or higher value, capped at five items per bill.

dailyNews.title: US’s Hegseth claims new Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured
dailyNews.description: Pete Hegseth's remarks come a day after Khamenei pledged to keep fighting in first statement since he was named leader.

dailyNews.title: Rahul Gandhi's big Kanshi Ram claim in UP Dalit outreach: 'Nehru would've made him Congress CM'
dailyNews.description: BSP under Kanshi Ram's protege, ex-CM Mayawati has tense relationship with Congress and SP, which are focusing on social justice agenda ahead of assembly polls

dailyNews.title: All six crew members killed after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
dailyNews.description: The US Central Command says all six crew members died after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq.

dailyNews.title: Cuba says it will release 51 prisoners in the coming days
dailyNews.description: Havana says it is in talks with Washington as no fuel has entered the island in three months.

dailyNews.title: US’s new scramble for Africa is biomedical imperialism
dailyNews.description: From Zimbabwe to Zambia, US health deals are raising fears of a new era of biomedical extractivism.

dailyNews.title: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claims Iran’s new Supreme Leader ‘likely wounded and disfigured’
dailyNews.description: Hegseth criticized Khamenei’s first statement for being written rather than delivered via video or audio and said there was no proof of his condition.

dailyNews.title: 'Mormon Wives' star weighs just 99 pounds at 5'10", admits GLP-1 addiction
dailyNews.description: Layla Taylor reveals her eating disorder struggle on "Mormon Wives." She weighs just 99 pounds at 5'10" and says she's unable to stop destructive patterns.

dailyNews.title: UNC GM Michael Lombardi claims 'fake stories' helped undermine Bill Belichick’s debut season
dailyNews.description: UNC general manager Michael Lombardi defended Bill Belichick's program against what he called "fake rumors" that overshadowed the team in the coach's first season.

dailyNews.title: ODU gunman who killed ROTC instructor had prior ISIS conviction, was released early
dailyNews.description: Accused ODU shooter was previously convicted for ISIS support but released early in 2024 after serving reduced sentence, court documents show.

topStories.title: Trump vows to hit Iran 'very hard' after obliterating nearly '90 percent' of regime missiles
topStories.description: President Donald Trump outlines continued Iran war strategy, claims 90% of Iranian missiles destroyed and signals more strikes as tensions drive oil prices higher.

topStories.title: Pete Hegseth Says ‘the Sooner David Ellison’ Buys CNN, ‘the Better’
topStories.description: The remarks fueled concerns that CNN’s prospective new owner may shift its coverage in a Trump-friendly direction.

topStories.title: Cuba Acknowledges Talks with Trump Officials For the First Time
topStories.description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel, whose country is rapidly running out of fuel, said the talks were based on “respect for the political systems of both countries.”

topStories.title: His Harvard Lab Was Thriving. Then Came the Cuts.
topStories.description: Will Mair, who studies aging, lost almost all his research funds when the White House cracked down on Harvard. He was wholly unprepared for the upheaval that followed.

topStories.title: When a President Gets Addicted to Regime Change
topStories.description: Venezuela gave Trump a taste of success. This isn’t the first time an American president has gotten hooked on overthrowing foreign governments.

topStories.title: Mussolini Would Have Loved Trump’s Ballroom
topStories.description: Trump’s plans for Washington bring to mind what Mussolini did — and tried to do — to Rome.

topStories.title: 6 more U.S. deaths, vague threat from Trump as Iran war nears 2-week mark
topStories.description: The U.S. military says six service members were killed in a plane crash in Iraq, as Iran's continued attacks on Gulf states keep oil prices high.

dailyNews.description: The U.S. military says six service members were killed in a plane crash in Iraq, as Iran's continued attacks on Gulf states keep oil prices high.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs Jones Act waiver amid rising fuel prices, White House says
dailyNews.description: Easing the century-old shipping law could help lower fuel prices as the Iran war pushes crude oil near $100 a barrel, experts say.

dailyNews.title: Trump reportedly told G7 leaders that Iran was about to surrender before supreme leader's address
dailyNews.description: President Trump told G7 leaders during a virtual meeting that Iran was "about to surrender," Axios reports. The claims apparently occurred before Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei's address emerged. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he thinks Putin is helping Iran
dailyNews.description: Russian leaders told President Trump they aren't sharing intelligence with Iran as it fights the U.S. and Israel, special envoy Steve Witkoff said this week.

dailyNews.title: U.S. companies should give Trump tariff refunds to workers as bonuses or raises: Greer
dailyNews.description: The United States government could end up paying $165 billion of more in refunds for Trump's IEEPA tariffs that the Supreme Court ruled were illegal.

dailyNews.title: Did tariff dividend checks just become more likely? Economists weigh in
dailyNews.description: Months after President Donald Trump suggested sending Americans a $2,000 tariff dividend check, there's a chance households could see that stimulus in 2026.

dailyNews.title: Cuba’s president confirms talks with U.S. — but warns an agreement will take time
dailyNews.description: It comes at a time when the Caribbean island is grappling with a worsening economic crisis.

dailyNews.title: Trump touted falling gas prices but now says 'we make a lot of money' as they go up
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump says all the time that gas prices are dropping on his watch, but with the Iran war, he says rising oil costs are good for America.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls U.S. operations in Iran 'a little excursion'
dailyNews.description: Trump calls U.S. operations in Iran 'a little excursion'

dailyNews.title: Trump expects military to do 'more of the same' in Iran
dailyNews.description: Trump expects military to do 'more of the same' in Iran

dailyNews.title: A 'little excursion' or 'war'? It's 'both,' Trump says: ANALYSIS
dailyNews.description: Amid rising gas prices and uncertainly at home about the cost and duration of the war with Iran, Trump is telling Americans this is only a short-term mission.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.
